book_claims.mechanism: Leucine is a potent activator of the mTOR/TOR-S6K signaling complex, which promotes cell growth and accelerates aging when chronically elevated

supplementary_research.finding: Dietary leucine restriction extends lifespan and healthspan in mice by reducing mTORC1 signaling and improving metabolic flexibility.
Chronic leucine supplementation accelerates cellular senescence markers via sustained mTOR activation; episodic leucine intake shows less aging impact.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Highest mTOR-activating BCAA; bioavailability near 100% from animal sources
Even small doses (1-3g) trigger robust mTOR activation; chronic excess linked to accelerated aging
Threshold ~0.7g per meal for muscle protein synthesis; context-dependent benefit vs. aging risk

synergies.description: Plant-based leucine from legumes provides episodic mTOR stimulation without chronic excess
Oleic acid moderates mTOR signaling, partially buffering leucine's pro-aging effects
Fish provides leucine alongside omega-3s that modulate inflammatory pathways downstream of mTOR
